opportunities.title: 622 CVEs? 570? Nobody Can Even Agree How Bad Patch Tuesday Was — And That's the Real Story
opportunities.why_now: The Register clocks last month's Patch Tuesday at a record 622 CVEs while Krebs reports 570, and Dark Reading says the record is already being broken again — the discrepancy itself signals how unmanageable triage has become.
opportunities.angle: Data-driven breakdown comparing the three counts, why methodology diverges, and a practical 'what to actually patch first' triage list for overwhelmed sysadmins

opportunities.title: LegacyHive: Microsoft's 'Serial Tormentor' Drops Another Zero-Day, and We're Grading It on a Curve
opportunities.why_now: A fresh zero-day dubbed LegacyHive is being actively exploited days after disclosure, with Register and SecurityWeek both covering the same actor's repeat offenses.
opportunities.angle: Threat-actor profile format tracking this group's pattern of Windows zero-days, what LegacyHive actually does, and why 'not as bad as promised' is cold comfort

opportunities.title: CISA Says Drop Everything: Three SharePoint Flaws Are Being Exploited Right Now
opportunities.why_now: CISA's active-exploitation alert on a trio of SharePoint vulnerabilities is a rare same-day 'patch now' signal echoed across Register and SecurityWeek within 24 hours.
opportunities.angle: Urgent patch-now briefing: CVE IDs, exploitation evidence, affected versions, and a one-page remediation checklist for IT teams

opportunities.title: Qantas Didn't Get Hacked — Someone Just Called the Help Desk
opportunities.why_now: The Qantas breach traces back to a tech-support scam, not a sophisticated exploit, proving once again that the weakest link wears a headset, not firewalls.
opportunities.angle: Breach postmortem focused on the social-engineering vector, with a sidebar on how airlines' outsourced support desks are becoming a favorite attack surface

opportunities.title: Forget Zero-Days: Stolen Logins Are Now Ransomware's Favorite Front Door
opportunities.why_now: Dark Reading's new data shows identity-based attacks have overtaken software exploits as the top ransomware entry point, flipping the conventional 'patch faster' narrative.
opportunities.angle: Trend analysis connecting this shift to the same week's law-firm 'one password to rule them all' fiasco, arguing patch cadence alone won't save you without identity hygiene
